rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion To Confirm Defendant Christie Wests Ownership Of 1215 29th Avenue San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2026. Line 1. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBINS Motion To Confirm Defendant Christie Wests Ownership Of 1215 29th Avenue San Francisco is continued to March 19, 2026 to be heard with other pending motion(s). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALEX ANDRADE ET AL VS. KNOX SRO LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2026. Line 5. PLAINTIFF ANGEL LIU MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file as to Ms. Hoots. Proof of service purports to attest to e-service on unspecified date. No e-mail address for Ms. Hoots listed, just a mailing address. Assuming that the motion was served on the date of filing, the notice would not be sufficient under CCP 1013 or 1010.6. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. SIMONE ALDERETTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default, Vacate Default Judgment, Recall Writ Due To Plaintiff'S Counsel'S Violation Of Ccp 583.130 And Ethical Duties And Due To Lack Of Service Ccp 473(B), (D), 473.5)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2026. Line 13. DEFENDANT SIMONE ALDERETTE Motion To Set Aside Default, Vacate Default Judgment, Recall Writ Due To Plaintiff'S Counsel'S Violation Of Ccp 583.130 And Ethical Duties And Due To Lack Of Service Ccp 473(B), (D), 473.5) is GRANTED. The Default is set aside per Shapell SoCal Rental Properties, LLC v. Chico's FAS, Inc. (2022) 85 Cal.App.5th 198 and Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127. On January 16, 2026 Plaintiff's counsel was notified that Ms. Payton was representing the defendant "with regards to the [Notice to Perform Covenant or Quit." This is the same notice that forms the basis of the Complaint in this action. Plaintiff should have notified the Defendant's counsel of their intent to request a default based on the date Plaintiff believed the response was due. This warning would have provided defense with an opportunity to file a timely response (e.g. Answer, Demurrer, motion to Quash) on January 30, 2026. Therefore, there is sufficient grounds under CCP 473(b) to find surprise on the defense counsel's part re: entry of default without notice expected from opposing counsel per the above stated authorities. In light of the above ruling, the Court does not reach other grounds for the motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
